description:
**Problem**
Plan requires crash tests to verify recovery behavior after SIGKILL at various points.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §18 (crash tests)

**Design**
- Integration tests that:
  - Inject SIGKILL during append (after write before fsync, after fsync before index commit, etc.)
  - Assert recovery: no duplicate origin_seq, tail truncation correct, index rebuild correct
- Use fork() or subprocess to inject crashes
- Verify idempotency mapping survives

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Test kills daemon mid-append and verifies recovery
- [ ] No duplicate origin_seq after crash
- [ ] Tail truncation handles partial records
- [ ] Index rebuild correct after crash

**Files:** tests/crash_recovery.rs (new)
title: Cross-phase: Crash safety integration tests

acceptance_criteria:
**Acceptance**
- [ ] Notes summarizing both papers are captured in the bead (or linked), and key mechanisms chosen.
- [ ] Interleaving replay harness exists and is used by at least one replication e2e test.
- [ ] Harness can replay a captured trace deterministically and fail on divergence.
- [ ] Tests write only under ./tmp and pass with `cargo test --features slow-tests`.
description:
**Problem**
We do not have interleaving-aware testing for replication; subtle message-order bugs can slip through even when the model looks right.
design:
**Design**
- First: fully read the papers in order before coding.
  1) iMocket: Model Checking Guided Incremental Testing for Distributed Systems
     https://dl.acm.org/doi/10.1145/3728883
  2) ER-π: Exhaustive Interleaving Replay for Testing Replicated Data Library Integration
     https://people.cs.vt.edu/provakar/Middleware_25__ER_%F0%9D%9C%8B_.pdf
- Extract concrete mechanisms we can implement (interleaving capture, scheduling control, replay).
- Implement a targeted interleaving replay harness for beads replication tests: record message orderings for repl sessions + tailnet proxy, replay deterministically, and integrate into slow-tests/CI.

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content:
Notes from papers:

- iMocket: incremental model-checking-guided testing. Model checker generates traces for tests; when the model changes, only affected states are re-tested via incremental testing patterns. Uses a growth-based graph traversal algorithm to identify affected states and minimize the required tests compared to full re-checking.

- ER-π: exhaustive interleaving replay for replicated libraries. Workflow: instrument Start/End around the code segment; proxy intercepts library calls to extract operations + events; generate interleavings, reset replicas between runs, and enforce a chosen interleaving via a distributed lock. Pruning techniques include event grouping, replica-specific pruning, event-independence pruning, and skipping failed ops.

Chosen mechanisms to implement now:
- Intercept replication frames via tailnet proxy, record the observed interleaving order, and replay deterministically.
- Deterministic replay enforcement with a strict order (fails if the run diverges).
- Keep trace files per link under ./tmp; integrate in a slow e2e test.

title: Realtime: interleaving replay harness (read iMocket + ER-π first)

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Retry never joins unrelated local/remote histories without `--force`, even after a retryable push rejection.
- [ ] A stale clone that migrates after another clone already pushed the canonical winner realigns locally without creating a duplicate migration commit.
- [ ] Retry tests cover an unrelated-history race and a converged remote-winner no-op case.
description:
**Problem**
The retry path can bypass hard-cutover safety rules. After a retryable push rejection, migration currently drops the unrelated-history refusal guard, so a remote force-push to unrelated `refs/heads/beads/store` between attempts can be joined without `--force`. Separately, a stale clone can mint an unnecessary follow-on migration commit even when another clone already migrated and pushed the canonical remote winner.

Both behaviors violate the hard-cutover design’s safety and no-op contracts.

**Files**: crates/beads-git/src/sync.rs, crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/cli/migration.rs, docs/plans/2026-03-01-legacy-deps-hard-cutover-design.md
design:
**Design**
Keep the unrelated-history refusal policy invariant across retries unless `--force` is explicitly set. Refetch on retry, recompute ancestry, and refuse unrelated divergence on every attempt.

Also make the no-op decision relative to the chosen parent tree after ref resolution. If the fetched remote winner already matches the would-be migrated snapshot, align local refs and return a genuine no-op instead of creating a duplicate migration commit.
title: Migration follow-up: preserve unrelated-history safety and true no-op convergence across retries
